She's not just pruning roses; she's pruning your team. Our deep dive into Hortensia explores her unique Flytrap Fiends that eat away at max health and her devastating hit-3 potential. Is she the answer to the minion meta? Find out inside.
Hortensia, The Fosterer of Flytraps: Pruning Minions and Planting Nightmares

Hortensia is a Legendary Nature hero from the Morlovia (Halloween) family, introduced in October 2025. She arrives as a direct answer to the overgrown garden of minion and mega-minion summoners that have taken root in the meta. As a Druid, she brings a natural resilience to the battlefield, but don't let her green thumb fool you—she's here to do serious damage. With a kit designed to strip away defenses and leave enemies withering, she’s a gardener who definitely doesn't believe in catch and release.

Hortensia’s design is a delightful blend of cottagecore and little shop of horrors. She sports a classic witchy brimmed hat adorned with autumn foliage, and she’s lovingly feeding a steak to a carnivorous plant that looks like it has a serious attitude problem. The vibrant colors and the juxtaposition of her cheerful expression with the monstrous flora make for a striking image. She looks like the kind of neighbor who wins the prize for biggest pumpkin but whose mailman mysteriously went missing.

provides a solid sponge for damage, ensuring she sticks around long enough to tend to her garden. She is competitively statted and capable of standing toe-to-toe with modern threats.

430% x 1297 Attack = ~5577 raw damage value per target. Hitting three targets, she outputs a massive amount of pressure.
A target with 2500 HP will receive a fiend that absorbs 1125 healing. That effectively negates a standard healer's entire special skill for that hero.
65 HP per turn might seem small, but over 5 turns, that's 325 permanent health loss. Against boosted health teams, this scales up, punishing overhear strategies.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family has evolved to keep up with the times, and Hortensia benefits greatly from her lineage.

The mana generation bonus is excellent. Even a single Morlovia hero gets a boost, but stacking her with someone like Alucard or a costumed Francine can ramp this up significantly. This flexibility allows you to fine-tune her tile requirements.

passive is a high-reward mechanic. A 65% chance to steal the newest dispellable buff is fantastic for disrupting the enemy's momentum. If there are no buffs to steal, she simply deals extra damage (150% to all), ensuring the passive is never wasted. It's a win-win scenario every time she fires.

is perfectly thematic and highly practical. In a meta filled with "vegetable" heroes and other fiend summoners, being immune to their mana-slowing or damage-dealing parasites ensures Hortensia keeps doing her job without interruption.

The Halloween event has always produced strong heroes (Miriam & Midnight, Alucard), and Hortensia continues this tradition. She is designed specifically to counter the heavy minion/mega-minion meta of 2024-2025.

Her hit-3 damage percentage (430%) is very high for Average speed, comparable to older slow heroes but with much better stats. The fiend mechanics introduce a new layer of "max health rot" that wasn't present in older heroes.
She is a premier counter to minion-heavy defenses and a strong flank attacker. While not a game-breaking tank, she is a devastating offensive tool and a solid defensive flank.
This review focuses on the base version of Hortensia, as she does not currently have a costume.
As a Druid, Hortensia has access to the Companion talent (minion summon), which adds a layer of meat shield. For her stats, you have two main schools of thought.
Maximizes the 430% damage multiplier. Her primary job is to kill or severely maim three enemies; this build ensures she does that effectively.
Slightly less bulk, making her more vulnerable to fast snipers.
Increases the durability of her Druid minions and keeps her alive longer to let the Flytrap Fiend's max health reduction do its work.
Sacrifices significant kill pressure on her special skill.
path is recommended because 430% damage is too good to pass up. You want her to shatter the enemy lines, not just annoy them.
430% damage is elite for a hit-3 Average hero.
Instantly removes meat shields before applying damage.
The 45% max health absorption is one of the thickest fiends in the game.
Max health reduction prevents enemies from stalling out the game indefinitely.
Healers who prioritize "destroying fiends" (like Esme or specialized cleansers) can negate her secondary effects.
Average speed can be too slow against a board of Very Fast Rush heroes without mana support.
The max health reduction takes time to become lethal; it's not an instant win button.
She is the queen of these modes. Minions become a liability for the enemy.
Excellent against teams relying on Mega Minions or heavy healers.
Fiends cannot be healed off in Bloody Battle (unless destroyed by a specific skill), making them permanent parasites.

Clears waves efficiently and the max HP reduction is great against high-HP bosses.
Hortensia thrives when paired with heroes who can exploit the holes she punches in the enemy defense.

While the fiend itself needs healing to remove, the max HP reduction is tied to the fiend. If the fiend goes, the effect stops (though the lost HP doesn't return).
Some heroes specifically counter minion/fiend summoners or resist them.

If you pull Hortensia early, focus on her immediately. She is a reliable damage dealer who will carry you through map stages and keep you competitive in raids. Don't worry too much about the complex fiend mechanics; just enjoy the fact that she hits like a truck.

Hortensia is a tactical nuke against the specific "Vegetable" and Mega Minion meta. She is worth the tonics and even Limit Break materials if you lack a strong hit-3 Nature hero. Her stats are modern enough to justify the investment, and her unique Max HP reduction offers a win condition against stall teams.
The community has dubbed the current era the "Vegetable Meta" (referring to the cartoonish plant heroes), and Hortensia is seen as the gardener sent to tidy up.
"She destroys minions AND hits for 430%? That's not a gardener, that's an exterminator."
"Pair her with a heal blocker and that fiend is basically a death sentence."
There is some debate about the 65 HP reduction being "too slow," but as one veteran countered,
"It adds up. In a 10-turn war battle, that's 650 health gone forever. That wins games."

Hortensia is a beautifully designed hero who fits perfectly into the modern game. She solves a specific problem (minions) while providing general utility (massive damage). She isn't just a niche counter; she's a powerhouse in her own right. Whether you're facing an army of undead minions or just a really stubborn healer, Hortensia has the tools to prune the competition back to the roots.
A must-level for anyone tired of seeing their damage soaked up by minions. She puts the "Power" in Flower Power.
